framework.name: Transferred Intent Doctrine Applied to Agents
framework.description: Legal doctrine that a wrongdoer intending to harm Person A but accidentally harming Person B can still be held liable. In agentic commerce, if Agent A intends to transact with Seller X but due to error transacts with Seller Y, existing legal frameworks may assign intent in unexpected ways. This creates settlement and dispute resolution challenges.
framework.raw_quote: transferred intent : intent attributed to a person who intends to cause another harm when the harm is accidentally inflicted on an unintended victim
